money_flow_summary: Money flows from employers through HR software (Workday, BambooHR), staffing agencies (15-30% of salary), and background check/payroll processors. HR is fundamentally an information and coordination function — identifying, screening, and managing people — which AI can automate across every stage. Solo operators can now build AI-native HR tools that undercut legacy vendors by 80% on price.

top_opportunities.title: AI Employee Onboarding Automation Platform
top_opportunities.how_money_is_made: Build a platform that automates the entire new hire onboarding: generates personalized Day 1-90 plans, auto-assigns training modules, collects required documents, schedules intro meetings with team members, and sends daily check-in prompts. Integrates with HRIS, Slack, and Google Workspace. Charge $199-$599/mo for teams of 10-200. Poor onboarding costs companies 20% of salary in lost productivity.
top_opportunities.moat: Saved onboarding templates per company create switching cost; integration with Slack/Google creates workflow dependency
top_opportunities.first_step: Build Slack bot that delivers daily onboarding tasks to new hires; integrate with Google Calendar for meeting scheduling; offer free 30-day trial via HR LinkedIn groups
top_opportunities.ai_agent_angle: AI personalizes the onboarding plan based on role, team, and manager — generating a custom 90-day plan that previously required an HR manager to write manually

top_opportunities.title: HR Policy & Employee Handbook Generator
top_opportunities.how_money_is_made: Build a tool that generates a complete, legally reviewed employee handbook from company details (size, state, industry, benefits). Updates automatically when employment law changes (minimum wage increases, new leave requirements). Charge $99-$299/mo. Law firms charge $3,000-$15,000 to draft handbooks; your tool does it in 5 minutes and keeps it current.
top_opportunities.moat: Legal update monitoring creates ongoing value that one-time document drafting doesn't; 50-state compliance library takes time to build
top_opportunities.first_step: Build handbook template system covering all 50 US state labor laws; create state + company size → policy rules engine; launch on HR Facebook groups
top_opportunities.ai_agent_angle: AI monitors state and federal employment law changes and automatically updates affected policy sections — a task that currently requires paying an employment attorney $300/hr to review annually

top_opportunities.title: AI Interview Intelligence & Scorecard Automation
top_opportunities.how_money_is_made: Build a tool that records interviews (with consent), transcribes, extracts structured scores against a customizable rubric, generates a debrief summary, and writes a Greenhouse/Lever ATS candidate note. Hiring managers currently spend 30-60 min writing debrief notes after each interview. Charge $299-$999/mo per hiring team. Reduces interview-to-offer cycle by 2-3 days.
top_opportunities.moat: Company-specific rubric and historical decision data creates hiring intelligence that improves over time
top_opportunities.first_step: Build Zoom/Google Meet recording integration + Whisper transcription + scorecard generation; demo with 1 Series A startup hiring engineering roles
top_opportunities.ai_agent_angle: AI scores candidates against job-specific competencies from conversation transcripts — extracting structured evidence from unstructured dialogue that humans evaluate inconsistently
